meta.title: Mercedes E220 CDI E220d Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes‑Benz E220 CDI / E220d engine — OM611, OM646, OM651 and OM654. W210, W211, W212 and W213 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

@graph.name: Mercedes‑Benz E220 CDI / E220d Engine Era Map
Mercedes‑Benz E220 CDI / E220d Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes‑Benz E220 CDI / E220d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned engine typically costs £1,000–£7,000 supply only depending on generation: OM611 (£1,000–£2,500), OM646 (£1,500–£3,500), OM651 (£2,000–£5,000), OM654 (£2,500–£7,000). Fitting adds £800–£1,400. Timing chain replacement on the OM651 can cost £1,500–£3,500 if caught early. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes‑Benz E220 CDI / E220d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the generation. The OM611 and OM646 (W210/W211) are rated ★★★☆☆ — robust but ageing. The OM651 (W212) is rated ★★★☆☆ — refined but with timing chain and injector issues. The OM654 (W213) is rated ★★★½☆ — the most modern and reliable, but AdBlue system faults are a watchpoint.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes‑Benz E220 CDI / E220d?

acceptedAnswer.text: OM611 injection pump and ECU issues; OM646 timing chain stretch; OM651 timing chain stretch and injector failure; OM654 AdBlue system failures (NOx sensors, pump, injector). All engines can suffer from EGR and DPF issues.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes‑Benz E220 CDI / E220d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well‑kept W212 E220 CDI worth £4,000–£8,000, a £3,500 recon is a significant investment — assess carefully. For a W213 E220d worth £10,000+, replacement is usually viable. For older W210/W211 cars, assess the body and running gear carefully.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes‑Benz E220 CDI / E220d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The E220 CDI uses OM611 (W210 1997–2002), OM646 (W211 2002–2009), OM651 (W212 2009–2016). The E220d uses OM654 (W213 2016–2023). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the OM651's timing chain, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A timing chain and tensioner replacement (£1,500–£3,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£2,000–£5,000 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The chain is located at the rear of the engine, so it's a labour‑intensive job, but it's far cheaper than a new engine.

eraMap.title: The E220 CDI / E220d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Early CDI — robust when maintained; injector and ECU issues are the main watchpoints.
The core E220 CDI generation — timing chain stretch and injector failure are the defining weaknesses.
The W212's OM651 — timing chain stretch, injector failure and DPF issues are well documented.
The modern E220d — OM654 with less chain issues, but AdBlue system and timing chain tensioner are watchpoints.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz E220 CDI / E220d Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an E220 diesel engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Timing chain replacement on the OM651 is a labour‑intensive job requiring gearbox removal.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-Benz E220 CDI / E220d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: If caught early; gearbox removal required but cheaper than replacement
Replace failed injectors; use updated parts
Replace leak‑off pipes; don't ignore diesel smell
Replace NOx sensors, pump or injector; don't let countdown expire
Regular cleaning; use quality fuel and motorway runs to prevent
Preventative — regular motorway runs are the best prevention
Replace pump; regular filter changes prevent recurrence

buyingChecks.title: Buying This E220 Diesel? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Mercedes service history with documented oil changes
* W213 OM654 if you want the most modern and reliable engine
* Evidence of timing chain work on OM651 cars
* No AdBlue system issues (W213) or evidence of NOx sensor replacement
avoidIf: * Cold‑start rattle with no timing chain history (OM651) — the single most important check
* Diesel smell from the engine bay — potential injector leak
* AdBlue countdown active (W213) — potential system failure
* Poor service history on any generation
* Injector replacement on W212 E220s is common, with Mercedes forums advising avoidance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The E220 CDI / E220d is the core diesel E-Class — and the engine choice determines your ownership experience. The OM611 and OM646 are robust but ageing. The OM651 is refined but has timing chain and injector issues. The OM654 is the most modern and reliable, but AdBlue system faults are a watchpoint. Buy on documented history, and if you're looking at a W212, demand chain‑history proof before you commit.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes‑Benz E220 CDI / E220d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling noise on startup (timing chain warning on OM651 — the most critical check)
* Service History — Including which engine era (OM611 for W210, OM646 for W211, OM651 for W212, OM654 for W213) — see the Era Map above
* Diesel Smell — Any strong diesel smell from the engine bay? Injector leak‑off pipe or injector failure
* AdBlue System — Any AdBlue warnings? Countdown active? NOx sensor or pump failure (W213)
* DPF Status — Any DPF warning lights or regeneration issues?
* EGR System — Any loss of power or black smoke? EGR clogging indicator
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, injector, or emissions issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures; often point to EGR, DPF, or AdBlue problems

items.label: Most Common Failure
items.value: OM651 timing chain stretch and injector failure — approximately 55% of OM651 enquiries; AdBlue issues on OM654 [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Peak Enquiry Season
items.value: Winter — cold starts expose timing chain issues [PATTERN DATA]
